questions.question: If the area of a parallelogram is 508 m2 and its base is 40 m, then what is the corresponding height of the parallelogram?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e 12.7 We know that the area (A) of a parallelogram is given by the formula: A = base x height height = A / base height = 508 m2 / 40 m height = 12.7 m

questions.question: A and B can complete a piece of work in 10 and 16 days respectively. A begins to do the work and they work alternatively one at a time for one day each. The whole work will be completed in :
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. \(12\frac{1}{4} days\) Calculations: Total work (LCM of 10 and 16) = 80 units Efficiency of A = 80/10 = 8 Efficiency of B = 80/16 = 5 Work done in 2 days, if they work alternatively starting with A ⇒ 2 days = 13 unit work ⇒ 12 days = 78 unit work Remaining work is completed by A = 2/8 = 1/4 Hence total time = 12 + 1/4 = 12(1/4) days

questions.question: The average of 8 numbers is 24. If one of the numbers is excluded, the average becomes 22. Find the excluded number.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 38. Average = Sum of observations/Number of observations The sum of 8 numbers = 24 × 8 = 192 After one number, excluding the average, it becomes 22 The sum of the remaining 7 numbers = 22 × 7 = 154 Hence, The excluded number is 192 - 154 = 38

questions.question: A runner runs a 750-metre race in 54 seconds. Find the runner’s speed (in km/h).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 50. Given that A runner runs a 750-metre race in 54 seconds Formula used Speed = Distance/Time Calculation ⇒The runner’s speed = 750/54 m/sec =(750/54) × 18/5 = 50 km/h

questions.question: Simplify the following expression: \({\left( {3\over a}+3a \right)}{\left( {9\over a^2}-9+9a^2 \right)}\ \)if a = 1
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 54. Given, a = 1 ⇒ \({\left( {3\over 1}+3(1) \right)}{\left( {9\over 1^2}-9+9(1)^2 \right)}\ \) ⇒ (3 + 3)(9 - 9 + 9) ⇒ 6 × 9 = 54

questions.question: In an election between A and B, every fifth vote polled was marked as invalid by the machine. In the remaining votes, A wins the election with a margin of 2500 votes, or 10% of the total votes polled, over B. If 90% of the invalid votes had been in favor of B, then which of the following would have been the result of the election?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. B would have won by 8%. Let V represent the total votes polled. Since every fifth vote is marked as invalid, the invalid votes are V/5 Therefore, The valid votes = V - V/5 = 4V/5 Candidate A wins by a margin of 2500 votes, which is also given as 10% of the total votes So, 10% of V ⇒ 10V/100 = 2500 ⇒ V = 25000 Therefore, the total votes polled, V, is 25000. Invalid Votes = V/5 = 25000/5 = 5000 Valid Votes = 4V/5 = 4 × 25000/5 = 20000 Given that A wins by 2500 votes Valid votes of A - valid votes of B = 2500.....(1) Also, the sum of valid votes for A and B is 20000 Valid votes of A + valid votes of B = 20000......(2) from eq 1 and 2 we get, ⇒ 2 × Valid votes of A = 22500 Valid votes of A = 11250 then, Valid votes of B = 20000 - 11250 = 8750 So, 90% of the invalid votes would have gone to B = 0.9 × 5000 = 4500 If these 4500 votes were added to B's total, B’s new total = 4500 + 8750 = 13250 Since A's total remains 11250, B would now have more votes than A. New margin = 11250 - 13250 = -2000 Percentage = New margin/Total votes × 100 ⇒ -2000/25000 × 100 ⇒ -8% So, B would have won by 8%.

questions.question: If \(sec\theta=\frac{4}{3}\), what is the value of tan2θ + tan4θ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. \(\frac{112}{81}\). We know that sec2θ - 1 = tan2θ = (4/3)2 - 1 = 7/9 the value of tan2θ + tan4θ ⇒ tan2θ(1 + tan2θ) = tan2θ × sec2θ ⇒ 7/9 × 16/9 = \(\frac{112}{81}\)

questions.question: Study the given table and answer the question that follows. The table shows the number of employees in three organizations during three years. Year/Organization 2022 2023 2024 P 75 69 85 Q 96 81 74 R 85 63 90 What is the respective ratio between the total number of employees in organizations P and R together in 2023 and the total number of employees in organizations Q and R together in 2024?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 33 : 41. The total number of employees in organisations P and R together in 2023 = 69 + 63 = 132 The total number of employees in organisations Q and R together in 2024 = 74 + 90 = 164 Required ratio = 132 : 164 = 33 : 41

questions.question: The perimeters of two similar triangles ABC and PQR are 156 cm and 46.8 cm, respectively. If BC = 19.5 cm and QR = x cm, then the value of x is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 5.85 cm. Perimeter of the triangle (ABC/PQR) = BC/QR = 156/46.8 = 19.5/x x = 6.5 × 0.9 = 5.85

questions.question: In a mixture, the ratio of a chemical X and water is 15 : 9. When 48 litres of this mixture is replaced with water, the ratio becomes 11 : 13. Find the quantity of water after replacement.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 97.5 liters. Let the total quantity of the initial mixture be 8k liters (5k of chemical X and 3k of water) The quantity of chemical X = (15/24) × 48 = 30 litres The quantity of water = (9/24) × 48 = 18 litres So, (5k - 30)/(3k + 30) = 11/13 ⇒ 65k - 390 = 33k + 330 ⇒ 32k = 720 ⇒ k = 720 / 32 = 22.5 ⇒ 3k + 30 = 3(22.5) + 30 = 67.5 + 30 = 97.5 Therefore, the quantity of water after replacement is 97.5 litres.

questions.question: A solid metallic cube of side 4.4 cm is melted and recast in the form of a wire of radius 2 mm. Find the length (in cm) of the wire (Use = π = 22/7)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 677.6 . A side of cube = 4.4 cm The radius of wire = 2 mm = 0.2 cm Let, the length of wire = h cm Volume of a cube = Volume of a metallic wire 4.43 = π × r2 × h = (22/7) × 0.22 × h h = 22 × 4.4 × 7 = 677.6 cm

questions.question: Pipes M, N, and S can fill a tank in 25, 50, and 100 minutes, respectively. Initially, pipes N and S are kept open for 10 minutes, and then pipe N is shut while pipe M is opened. Pipe S is closed 15 minutes before the tank overflows. How much time (in minutes) will it take to fill the tank if the three pipes work in this pattern?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 27. Pipes M, N, and S can fill a tank in 25, 50, and 100 minutes, respectively L.C.M(25, 50, and 100) = 100 The efficiency of M = 100/25 = 4 units The efficiency of N = 100/50 = 2 units The efficiency of S = 100/100 = 1 unit Initially, pipes N and S are kept open for 10 minutes, Work done by N and S in 10 minutes = (2 + 1) × 10 = 3 × 10 = 30 units Remaining work = 100 - 30 = 70 units M will work for 15 minutes, Work done by M in 15 minutes = 4 × 15 = 60 units Remaining work = 70 - 60 = 10 units Now, M and S work 10 units Time taken by M and S = 10/(4 + 1) = 10/5 = 2 minutes Thus, Total time taken = 10 + 15 + 2 = 27 minutes

questions.question: In a company, all employees are getting equal wages. If the number of employees is increased by 20% and their wages per head are decreased by 18%, what is the percentage decrease in total wages?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 1.6%. Let the initial number of employees = x The initial wage per employee = y So, the initial total wages = xy New number of employees = 1.20x New wage per employee = 0.82y New total wages = (1.20x)(0.82y) = 0.984xy Decrease in total wages= xy - 0.984xy = 0.016xy Percentage decrease = (0.016xy/xy) × 100 = 1.6% Therefore, the percentage decrease in total wages is 1.6%.

questions.question: Rs.26000 is split into two sums such that the Simple Interest for 5 years at 10% on one part equals the Simple Interest for 6 years at 9% on the other part. What is the sum placed at 10% for 5 years?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Rs.13500. Let the first part of the sum be x and the second part be (26000 - x). S.I. = (P × R × T)/100 here, S.I. = Simple Interest, P = principal, R = Rate, and T = Time The first part, ⇒ S.I. = (x × 10 × 5)/100 ⇒ S.I. = 50x/100 ⇒ S.I. = 0.5x The second part, ⇒ S.I. = ((26000 - x) × 9 × 6)/100 ⇒ S.I. = ((26000 - x) × 54)/100 ⇒ S.I. = 0.54(26000 - X) ⇒ S.I. = 14040 - 0.54x According to the question, the simple interest on both parts is equal, so ⇒ 0.5x = 14040 - 0.54x ⇒ 0.5x + 0.54x = 14040 ⇒ 1.04x = 14040 ⇒ x = 14040/1.04 ⇒ x = 13500 Thus, the sum placed at 10% for 5 years is Rs. 13,500

questions.question: Two circles of radii 10 cm and 5 cm touch each other externally at point A. PQ is the direct common tangent of those two circles of centers O1 and O2 respectively. The length of PQ is equal to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 10\(\sqrt{2}\) cm. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Gemetory_13%20aug-2024_D202024_08_13_06_03_55.PNG) The length of PQ = 2√(r1 × r2) ⇒ PQ = 2√(10 × 5) ⇒ PQ = 2√(5 × 2 × 5) ⇒ PQ = 10√2 cm

questions.question: A dealer fixed the price of an article 40% above the cost of production. While selling it he allows a discount of 20% and makes a profit of Rs. 72. Find the cost of production (in Rs.) of the article.
questions.explanation: The Correct answer is option 3 i.e. Rs.600. Let the cost of production be 100 units. Marked price = 100 + 40 = 140 units Selling price = Marked price × (100 - D)/100 Selling price = 140 × (100 - 20)/100 = (140 × 80)/100 = 112 units Profit = S.P. - C.P. ⇒ 112 units - 100 units = Rs. 72 ⇒ 12 units = 72 ⇒ 100 units = (72/12) × 100 = Rs. 600 Hence, the cost of the production is Rs. 600.

questions.question: Laxman marks his goods at 60% above the cost price and allows a discount of 20%. What is his gain/loss percentage?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Profit 28%. Profit = SP - CP SP = Selling price, CP = Cost price Profit% = (Profit/CP) × 100 Let the Cost price be 100x ⇒ Marked price = 160% of 100x = 160x Selling price after discount of 20% = 80% of 160x = 128x Profit = 128x - 100x = 28x Required Profit% = (28x/100x) × 100 = 28%

questions.question: AB is the diameter of a circle with the center O. P as a point on it. If ∠AOP = 95° then find ∠OBP ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 47.5°. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Gemetory_13%20aug-2024_D352024_08_13_07_02_13.PNG) ∠AOP = 95° ∠BOP = 180° - 95° = 85° OP = OB = radius = r So, triangle OBP is a isosceles triagnle ∠B = ∠P = x So, ∠BOP + ∠OBP + ∠OPB = 180° ⇒ 85° + x + x = 180° ⇒ x = 95°/2 = 47.5° ⇒ ∠OBP = 47.5°

questions.question: A buys 150 gm gold for Rs.6,00,000 and gives 6% as making charges. What should be the selling price per 10 gm to have a profit of 6%?
questions.explanation: The Correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rs.44,944. C.P. of 150 gm gold = Rs. 600000 C.P. of 10 gm gold = Rs. 6000000/150 = Rs. 40000 Making charges = 6% of 40000 = Rs. 2400 Total C.P. = 40000 + 2400 = Rs. 42400 Profit % = 6% S.P. = C.P. × (100 + P)/100 S.P. = 42400 × (100 + 6)/100 = 424 × 106 = Rs. 44944

questions.question: Solve the given set of three equations to get the value of the unknowns: x + 2z = 3 x + 2y + 3z = 5 3x - 5z = -13
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. x = -1,y = 0,z = 2. ⇒ x + 2z = 3.....(1) ⇒ x + 2y + 3z = 5.....(2) ⇒ 3x - 5z = -13.....(3) Solving eq 1 and 3 ⇒ x + 2z = 3 [multiply eq by 3] ⇒ 3x - 5z = -13 On solving ⇒ 11z = 22 ⇒ z = 2 put in eq 1 ⇒ x = -1 On putting the value of x and z in eq 2 ⇒ -1 + 2y + 6 = 5 ⇒ 2y = 0 ⇒ y = 0 Thus, the values of x, y, and z are -1, 0, and 2 respectively

questions.question: On what principle will the difference between Compound Interest and Simple Interest at a 10% rate in 3 years be equal to Rs.620?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Rs.20000. Difference = (P × R2)(300 + R)/1003 here, P = Principal, R = Rate ⇒ 620 = (P × 10 × 10)(300 + 10)/1003 ⇒ 620000000 = (P × 10 × 10)(310) ⇒ P = 6200000/310 ⇒ P = Rs.20000 Thus, the principal is Rs. 20,000.

questions.question: What number from the following should be deducted from 1184 to make it exactly divisible by 21?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 8. 1184 = 21 × 56 + 8 Here, 8 is a remainder so 8 should be deducted from 1184 to make it exactly divisible by 21.

questions.question: A solid metallic right circular cylinder has a diameter of 32 cm and a height of 9 cm. It is melted and recast into a solid sphere. What is the radius (in cm) of the sphere?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 12. Volume of cylinder = Volume of sphere Volume of cylinder = \(\pi r^2h\) Volume of sphere = \({4 \over 3} \pi r^3\) Volume of cylinder = \(\pi \times16^2 \times 9\) Volume of cylinder = Volume of sphere ⇒ \(\pi \times16^2 \times 9\) = \({4 \over 3} \pi r^3\) ⇒ r3 = 64 × 27 ⇒ r = 12 Hence the required answer is 12 cm.

questions.question: 40 men can complete a work in 15 days. Three days after they started working, 20 more men joined them. In how many days the total work will be completed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 11 Concept used: Work = Number of people × number of days Calculations: ⇒ Total work = 40 × 15 = 600 units 40 men worked for 3 days, work done = 40 × 3 = 120 units Remaining work = 600 - 120 = 480 units 20 more men joined the work, total men = 40 + 20 = 60 Time taken = 480/60 = 8 Total time = 3 + 8 = 11 days

questions.question: The table shows the number of wickets taken by each bowler with the number of wickets. No. of wickets per bowler No. of bowlers 1 4 2 3 3 6 4 5 5 2 Find the median of the number of wickets from the given table.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 3. No. of wickets No of bowlers Cumulative frequency 1 4 4 2 3 4 + 3 = 7 3 6 7 + 6 = 13 4 5 13 + 5 = 18 5 2 18 + 2 = 20 Cumulative mean = 20/2 = 10 Here, 10 lies in between 7 and 13. So, median = 3 Therefore, the median of the number of wickets is 3.

questions.question: The average of 29 observations is 40. If the average of the first 15 observations is 38, and that of the last 15 observations is 45, then the 15th observation is:
questions.explanation: The Correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 85. Average = Sum of observations/Number of observations Sum of 29 observations = 29 × 40 = 1160 Sum of first 15 observations = 15 × 38 = 570 Sum of last 15 observations = 15 × 45 = 675 Hence, 15th observation will be = (570 + 675) - 1160 ⇒ 1245 - 1160 = 85

questions.question: The sum of two digits of a number is 10. If the digits are interchanged, then its value increases by 18. Find the number.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 46. Let the number be 10x + y. ⇒ x + y = 10 ..... (1) Now, ⇒ [10y + x] - [10x + y] = 18 ⇒ 9y - 9x = 18 ⇒ y - x = 2 ..... (2) from eq. (1) and (2), ⇒ 10 - x - x = 2 ⇒ 2x = 8 ⇒ x = 4 y = 6 So, the number is 46.

questions.question: How many prime numbers are there between the positive integers 60 and 100?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 8. 60 To 100 Prime numbers are: 61, 67, 71, 73, 79, 83, 89 and 97 Here, the total 8 prime numbers lie between 60 and 100.

questions.question: A man observed the top of an electric pole at angles of elevation of 60° and 30°, respectively, from two different observation points P and Q. If the distance between P and Q is 10 m, find the height of the pole (in m).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 5√3. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Black%20Diagram_19-09-2024_D32024_09_19_17_11_40.PNG) According to the question PQ = 10 m, So, In triangle APO , ⇒ tan 60º = H/OP ⇒ √3 = H/OP ⇒ OP = H/√3 m In triangle AOQ, ⇒ tan 30º = H/OQ ⇒1/√3 = H/OQ ⇒ OQ = H√3 m OQ - OP = PQ ⇒ H√3 - H/√3 = 10 ⇒ 3H - H = 10√3 ⇒ 2H = 10√3 ⇒ H = 5√3 m

questions.question: If A : B = 5 : 4, B : C = 8 : 5 and C : D = 3 : 1. Find A : D.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 6: 1. A : B = 5 : 4 B : C = 8 : 5 C : D = 3 : 1 A : B = 120 : 96 [Multiply by 8 × 3 = 24] B : C = 96 : 60 [Multiply by 4 × 3 = 12] C : D = 60 : 20 [Multiply by 5 × 4 = 20] A : B : C : D = 120 : 96 : 60 : 20 A : D = 120 : 20 = 6 : 1

questions.question: In a college union, there are 48 students. The ratio of the number of boys to the number of girls is 5 : 3. The number of girls to be added in the union, so that the number of boys to girls in 6 : 5, is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 7. Number of boys = 48 × 5/8 = 30 Number of girls = 48 – 30 = 18 So, 30/(18 + x) = 6 : 5 ⇒ 108 + 6x = 150 ⇒ 6x = 42 ⇒ x = 7 Hence, 7 girls should be added.

questions.question: A person covers 28 km while walking at a speed of 8 km/h. How much distance (in km) will he cover in same time if he walks at a speed of 6 km/h?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 21. Speed = distance/time Given that A person covers 28 km while walking at a speed of 8 km/h if he walks at a speed of 6 km/h, According to the question For constant time, Speed is proportional to the Distance Hence, the required distance = 28 × 6/8 = 21 km

questions.question: Three partners A, B, and C start a business. Twice of A's capital is equal to thrice of B's capital and B's capital is four times C's capital. Out of a total profit of Rs. 15,400 at the end of the year, C's share is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Rs 1400. Twice of A's capital is equal to thrice of B's capital and B's capital is four times C's capital 2A = 3B ⇒ A : B = 3 : 2 B = 4C ⇒ B : C = 4 : 1 So, A : B : C = 6 : 4 : 1 C's profit share = 15400 × (1/11) = Rs. 1400

questions.question: Directions: A question is given, followed by two statements labelled I and II. questions.question: Rajan got married 8 years back. His age then was 5/6 of his present age. His sister was 10 years younger at the time of his marriage. How old is she now?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 38. Let Rajan's present age = A Rajan's age at the time of marriage = A - 8 Rajan's sister's age at the time of his marriage = A - 8 - 10 = A - 18 Therefore, the sister's present age = A - 10 According to the given information, A - 8 = 5/6A A - 5/6A = 8 1/6A = 8 A = 48 Sister's present age = 48 - 10 = 38

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. ÷ and ×. Given equation: 5 + 6 ÷ 3 - 12 × 2 = 17 Option 1) ÷ and × By interchanging the signs, we get 5 + 6 × 3 - (12 ÷ 2) = 17 5 + (6 × 3) - 6 = 17 (5 + 18) - 6 = 17 23 - 6 = 17 17 = 17

questions.question: Six friends, named from A to F, are sitting in a row, facing north. A sits at the extreme left position. B sits one place away from the person sitting at the extreme right. C sits one place away from B, but not at the extreme right. D can't sit adjacent to B. E sits adjacent to C. Who sits in the 5th position from the left end?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. B. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_12-05-2025-D32025_05_12_19_20_31.png) So, B sits at the 5th position from the left end.

questions.question: Jagdish, from his house, ran 200 steps towards the east and then ran 40 steps after turning to his right to reach a tea stall. He then ran 40 steps toward his right and reached a bus stop. He then turned towards his right and ran 40 steps. From there, turning towards his right he ran 140 steps and reached a post office. How far and in which direction is the post office with respect to Jagdish’s house?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 300 steps, East. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_02-05-2025_D32025_05_02_19_41_20.png) The post office is 300 steps, East of Jagdish's house.

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following set. (21, 5, 49) (77, 9, 161) (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/deleting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. (60, 8, 127). Logic: (Second number)2 - 4 = First number; (First number × 2) + 7 = Third number (21, 5, 49) → 52 - 4 = 21, (21 × 2) + 7 = 49 (77, 9, 161) → 92 - 4 = 77, (77 × 2) + 7 = 161 Similarly, (60, 8, 127) → 82 - 4 = 60, (60 × 2) + 7 = 127

Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All roses are lotus. Some lotus are tulips. Some tulips are lilies. Conclusions: I. Some lotuses are rose II. All tulips are lilies. III. Some tulips are lotus.
options: Only conclusions II and III follow.
Only conclusions I and III follow.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Only conclusions I andI III follow. Some tulips are lotus. → Follows (As some lotus are tulips. So, some tulips are lotus too)

questions.question: The same operation(s) are followed in all the given number pairs except one. Find that odd number pair. (NOTE : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /deleting /multiplying etc., to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
At what time between 5 and 6 will the two hands of a watch coincide?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 27\(\frac{3}{11}\) minutes past 5. The angle between the hour hand and minute hand = 30H - 11M ÷ 2 H = 5, Angle = 0° 0 = 30 × 5 - 11 × M ÷ 2 0 = 150 - 11 × M ÷ 2 11 × M ÷ 2 = 150 M = 150 × 2 ÷ 11 M = 300 ÷ 11 M = 27\(\bf\frac{3}{11}\) Thus, the hour and minute hand coincide at 27\(\frac{3}{11}\) minutes past 5.

questions.question: Three statements are given followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: Some doors are silver. No silver is fish. All fish are dolphins. Conclusions: I. All dolphins can never be silver. II. All doors can never be fish. III. No door is a dolphin.
options: Only conclusion I and III follow
Only conclusion I and II follow
All conclusion I, II and III follow
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Only conclusion I and II follow. All dolphins can never be silver. → Follows (because some part of the dolphin is fish and no silver is fish) II. All doors can never be fish. → Follows (because the part of the doors that is silver can never be fish, as no silver is fish) III. No door is a dolphin. → Does not follow (It is possible but not definite)

questions.question: Direction: In the given statement, the relationship between different elements is shown and it is followed by three conclusions. Choose the correct answer based on the information given below. Statements: 25 26 ≥ 27; 26 ≤ 29 = 30 Conclusions: I. 29 ≥ 27 II. 24 > 27 III. 25
options: Both Conclusions I and II follow
Both Conclusions II and III follow
Both Conclusions I and III follow

Similarly, certain mathematical operation(s) on the second number result(s) in the third number and so on. Which of the given options follows the same set of operations as in the given sets? (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) 13 – 39 – 78 – 55; 19 – 57 – 114 – 91
If the 3rd day of a month is Monday, which of the following would be the 4th day before the 21st day of that month?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Monday. If the 3rd day of the month = Monday, 4th day before the 21st day of that month = 17th day Number of odd days between 3rd day and 17th day of the month = 14 ÷ 7 = 0 odd days Therefore, the 4th day before the 21st day of the month will also be Monday, as it will be the same day of the week as the 3rd day.

questions.question: If ‘QUERY’ is coded as ‘42’, ‘CROWN’ is coded as ‘17’, then what is the code for ‘ORDER’ in the same code language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 33. Logic: The words are coded as the sum of the positional value of 1st and last letter. As ‘QUERY’ is coded as ‘42’: (17 + 25) = 42 As ‘CROWN’ is coded as ‘17’: (3 + 14) = 17 Similarly, ORDER: (15 + 18) = 33

questions.question: In a row of girls, A is 10th from the left and B is 14th from the right. If they interchange their positions, A becomes 24th from the left. How many girls are there in a row?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 37. Total girls = Position from the left + Position from the right − 1 = 24 + 14 − 1 = 37.

options: Both conclusions I and II follow
Either conclusion I or II follows
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Either conclusion I or II follows. Statement: S = N Conclusion: I: Q False (Q = K ≤ G → Q ≤ G) II: Q = G: False (Q = K ≤ G → Q ≤ G) Conclusion I and II form complementary pairs, so Either Conclusion I or II follows.

questions.question: Directions: In this question, two statements I and II have been given. These statements may be independent causes or effects of independent causes or a common cause. One of the statements may be the effect of the other statement. Read both statements and select the correct answer. I. A sports company released a new line of cricket equipment. II. The meteorological department issued a cyclone warning in coastal areas.
options: I is the cause and II is the effect
II is the cause and I is the effect
Both are effects of a common cause
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Both are independent causes. These two statements refer to completely unrelated domains: One is about a commercial launch in the sports industry. The other is about a natural weather warning from a government body. Thus, Both are independent causes.

questions.question: The position of how many letters will remain unchanged if each of the letters in the word ‘OBSERVED’ is arranged in the English alphabetical order?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. One. Given word O B S E R V E D Alphabetical order B D E E O R S V Thus, the position of one letter will remain unchanged.

questions.question: Which company has Adani Total Gas Ltd (ATGL) recently formed a strategic partnership with to dispense each other’s fuels at select outlets, as announced on June 25, 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Reliance BP Mobility Ltd (Jio‑bp). Adani Total Gas Ltd (ATGL) and Reliance BP Mobility Ltd (Jio-bp) have formed a strategic partnership to dispense each other’s fuels at select outlets, announced on June 25, 2025. Under the deal, ATGL will offer Jio-bp petrol and diesel, while Jio-bp will host ATGL’s CNG units. This collaboration aims to enhance infrastructure use, improve customer experience, and expand private sector presence in a market where public firms control 90% of fuel outlets. It follows growing cooperation between Adani and Reliance.

questions.question: Which Union Minister led the Indian delegation at the SCO Defence Ministers recent meeting?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rajnath Singh. The Defence Ministers from all 10 member states of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) convened for a crucial meeting. Union Minister Rajnath Singh, representing the Ministry of Defence (MoD), led the Indian delegation at this significant gathering. This visit by an Indian Defence Minister to China marks a notable diplomatic event, as it is the first such visit since the Galwan Valley clash in June 2020. The meeting provided a platform for discussions on regional security, counter-terrorism efforts, and strengthening defence cooperation among the SCO member nations.

questions.question: The recently launched Dorjilung Hydropower Project is a joint venture between which two countries?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Bhutan and India. A significant milestone in regional clean energy collaboration has been achieved with the launch of the Dorjilung Hydropower Project in Bhutan. This 1125 MW project is a joint venture between Bhutan’s state-owned Druk Green Power Corporation (DGPC) and India's private sector giant, Tata Power Company Limited. Developed on the Kurichhu River, the run-of-the-river project is a part of the larger strategic partnership between the two nations to develop 5000 MW of renewable energy. With an estimated cost of $1.7 billion, the project is being financed by the World Bank. The partnership with Tata Power signifies a strategic shift by Bhutan towards seeking private investments rather than relying solely on government grants.

questions.question: When is the polling for the 2025 Vice-Presidential Election in India scheduled, if required?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. September 9. Following Jagdeep Dhankhar’s surprise resignation from the Vice President post on July 21, 2025, the Election Commission of India has set a timetable for the 17th Vice‑Presidential election. As per constitutional requirements, the official notification will be issued on August 7. Nominations close on August 21, scrutiny will be on August 22, with candidatures withdrawable until August 25. If necessary, polling and counting will take place on September 9, 2025, using a secret ballot with proportional representation through a single transferable vote system.

questions.question: When is World Lung Cancer Day observed each year to raise awareness, support early detection, and highlight prevention of this highly fatal disease?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. August 1. World Lung Cancer Day, marked annually on August 1, highlights the vital need for awareness, early detection, and prevention of one of the most lethal forms of cancer worldwide. Lung cancer frequently remains undetected until later stages and affects both smokers and non-smokers due to factors like air pollution, occupational exposure, radon gas, and genetics. Because early symptoms are often mild or overlooked, timely screening is vital. Advances in therapies such as immunotherapy and precision radiotherapy bring new hope, while awareness and myth-busting can greatly enhance survival rates.

questions.question: Cloves Syndrome Awareness Day is observed every year on which date?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. August 3. Cloves Syndrome Awareness Day is observed annually on August 3 to globally spread awareness about CLOVES syndrome, a rare and complex genetic disorder. Established by the CLOVES Syndrome Community in 2010, the day aims to increase knowledge about this congenital disease, which affects a small number of individuals worldwide. It is a day to build a sense of community, advocate for enhanced research and treatment options, and provide support to affected families who often face unique challenges due to the rarity of the condition. The observance encourages people to wear green, the color associated with Cloves syndrome, to show solidarity.

questions.question: The Akhil Bharatiya Shiksha Samagam 2025 was recently held to mark which significant milestone?
options: Establishment of HECI (Higher Education Commission of India)
Five years since the launch of NEP 2020
Completion of a ten years of digital education initiatives
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Five years since the launch of NEP 2020. Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan inaugurated the Akhil Bharatiya Shiksha Samagam 2025, celebrating five years since the launch of NEP 2020. The event saw the rollout of educational initiatives worth ₹4,000 crore, including enhanced school infrastructure and digital platforms like TARA and ViBe. Foreign universities expressed interest in establishing campuses in India. The summit highlighted progress in curriculum reforms, foundational learning, teacher development, and digital integration. However, challenges such as state-level policy disagreements and delays in institutional reforms like HECI and teacher education frameworks persist.

questions.question: The Kamchatka Peninsula experienced a powerful 8.8-magnitude earthquake, one of the strongest in the region since 1952. Which region does this earthquake pertain to?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Russia. On July 30, 2025, the Kamchatka Peninsula in Russia experienced a powerful 8.8-magnitude earthquake, one of the strongest in the region since 1952. The quake triggered tsunami warnings across the Pacific, prompting evacuations in areas including Japan, Hawaii, and Russia. Despite initial fears, the tsunami waves remained smaller than anticipated, and the warnings were lifted within hours. The earthquake also led to the eruption of the Klyuchevskoy volcano, Eurasia's highest active volcano, which began emitting lava and ash. While the eruption was visually dramatic, it posed little immediate threat to populated areas. The region's preparedness and swift response helped prevent significant damage or casualties.

questions.question: What is the primary focus of the recent efforts in Kaziranga National Park?
options: Comprehensive bird census for grassland species
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Comprehensive bird census for grassland species. Recent efforts in Kaziranga National Park have focused on a comprehensive bird census to assess the status of grassland bird species. This initiative is crucial as grassland birds are facing a severe crisis due to habitat degradation and encroachment, posing a threat to the park's overall biodiversity. The census aims to provide vital data for developing targeted conservation strategies and restoring the ecological balance within the park, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its unique ecosystem.

questions.question: DRDO’s recent missile tests at Dr APJ Abdul Kalam Island involved which indigenous quasi-ballistic missile?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Pralay. The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) successfully conducted range tests of the indigenous quasi-ballistic missile ‘Pralay’ over two days from Dr APJ Abdul Kalam Island in Odisha. The missile precisely struck its targets, confirming its minimum and maximum range capabilities. Developed collaboratively by several DRDO laboratories and industry partners such as Bharat Dynamics and Bharat Electronics, Pralay is equipped with advanced guidance systems and can carry different types of warheads. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh and DRDO Chairman Samir Kamat lauded the tests, calling them a significant milestone toward the missile’s induction into the Armed Forces.

questions.question: A/an _____ is a draft of a proposed legislative law.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Bill. A bill is a draft of a proposed legislative law. It is introduced in a legislature for discussion and approval. The process of turning a bill into law typically involves several stages, including committee review, debates, amendments, and votes. If both houses of the legislature approve the bill, it is sent to the executive (such as a president or governor) for final approval. If signed, the bill becomes law; if vetoed, the legislature may attempt to override the veto. This legislative process ensures thorough review before a bill becomes law.

questions.question: The Lok Sabha passed the Family Courts’ Amendment Bill on __________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. July 2022. The Lok Sabha passed the Family Courts (Amendment) Bill in July 2022. This amendment aims to enhance the functioning of family courts by enabling the establishment of more family courts across India. The bill also seeks to provide better access to justice for families, streamline the resolution of disputes, and ensure timely and effective delivery of justice in family-related matters. The passage of this bill is expected to improve the judicial process for family law cases.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about fundamental duties of the Indian Constitution is correct?
options: To preserve biodiversity is a fundamental duty
To make compulsory education to children of 8 to 14 years as our fundamental duty
To obey parents is a fundamental duty
To save money for future is a fundamental duty
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1, i.e. to preserve biodiversity is a fundamental duty. Under Article 51A of the Indian Constitution, one of the fundamental duties is to protect and improve the natural environment, which includes preserving biodiversity. While compulsory education for children between 6 to 14 years is a fundamental right, obeying parents and saving money are not explicitly mentioned as fundamental duties.

questions.question: Which of the following words is NOT mentioned in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Federalism. The word "Federalism" is not explicitly mentioned in the Indian Constitution. While the Constitution establishes a federal structure of governance through the distribution of powers between the central and state governments, the term itself does not appear in the text. The words "Secular," "Republic," and "Socialist" are mentioned, with "Secular" reflecting the state's neutrality toward all religions, "Republic" indicating a government where the head of state is elected, and "Socialist" emphasizing the goal of a just and equitable society.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution refers to the Rights of Ministers concerning the State Legislature?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Article 177. Article 177 of the Indian Constitution refers to the Rights of Ministers concerning the State Legislature. It provides that Ministers have the right to take part in the proceedings of the State Legislature, including speaking and making statements, but without the right to vote in the House. This ensures that Ministers can contribute to legislative discussions while maintaining a separation of powers between the legislative and executive branches. • Article 175 deals with the Governor's address. • Article 178 relates to the Speaker of the Legislative Assembly. • Article 176 discusses the Governor's special address.

questions.question: In which year was the rupee devalued for the first time by nearly 37%?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 1966. In 1966, India devalued its currency by nearly 37% for the first time. This decision was triggered by a combination of factors—economic instability caused by the Indo-China War (1962), Indo-Pak War (1965), a severe drought, and a rising import bill. The move was meant to encourage exports and attract foreign aid by making Indian goods cheaper globally. Extra Information: What is Devaluation? • A deliberate lowering of the currency's value in terms of foreign currency. • Makes exports cheaper and imports more expensive, helping correct the trade imbalance. History of Rupee Devaluation in India: • 1966: First devaluation—1 USD increased from ₹4.76 to ₹7.50. • 1991: Second major devaluation during a balance of payments crisis—1 USD rose to ₹24.58. • Post-1991: India moved to a market-determined exchange rate system. Reasons Behind Devaluation: • Economic crises, wars, heavy import dependency (especially oil), rising inflation, and IMF(International Monetary Fund) loan conditions. Impact of Devaluation: • Short-term inflation is due to costly imports. • Long-term benefits through increased exports and foreign capital inflow.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a stock variable?
options: Import of food grains in a particular financial year
Amount of food grains available in all warehouses of the country on the first day of its financial year
Annual production of food grains in a country
Export of food grains in a particular financial year
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Amount of food grains available in all warehouses of the country on the first day of its financial year • The amount of food grains in warehouses on a specific date is a stock variable. Stock variables are measured at a particular point in time, representing a quantity that exists at that instant. • The food grain inventory is not measured over a period, but rather as a snapshot of what's available on that first day.

questions.question: What is an association of workers for the purpose of maintaining or improving the conditions of their employment called as?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Trade Union. • A Trade Union is an organized association of workers formed to protect and promote their rights and interests, particularly in terms of wages, working conditions, benefits, and hours. • These unions are a key part of the labor movement, working towards improving the economic status and welfare of workers. • They can negotiate with employers and influence government policies to protect workers' rights. • A trade union can represent workers in collective bargaining, ensuring fair wages, safe working conditions, and other employment benefits. • Unions also offer legal support and solidarity to their members during disputes or conflicts with employers. • In many countries, trade unions play an important role in shaping labor laws and providing support for worker welfare. • Famous examples include the American Federation of Labor (AFL) and the All India Trade Union Congress (AITUC) in India.

questions.question: In which of the Five-Year Plans was ‘The Twenty Point Programme’ launched?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Fifth Five-Year Plan. The 'Twenty Point Programme' was launched in 1975 by then Prime Minister Indira Gandhi during the Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974–1979). Its objective was to eradicate poverty, reduce unemployment, and achieve socio-economic goals such as improvements in health, education, and agriculture. This programme has been one of the major government initiatives aimed at helping people living below the poverty line. • Sixth Plan (1980–1985): Focused on poverty eradication and technological development. • Seventh Plan (1985–1990): Prioritized productivity and public welfare. • Eighth Plan (1992–1997): Marked a significant shift towards liberalization and economic reforms.

questions.question: Which of the following committees gave regulatory recommendations for microfinance?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e.Malegam Committee, The Malegam Committee (2010) provided key regulatory recommendations for microfinance in India. It was set up by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to address concerns regarding the functioning of Microfinance Institutions (MFIs), especially issues related to high interest rates, coercive recovery practices, and over-indebtedness of borrowers. Key Recommendations : are the Creation of a separate category of NBFCs called NBFC-MFIs, regulated by the RBI, and an Interest rate cap to prevent MFIs from charging excessively high rates. Limits on borrower debt exposure, ensuring that an individual does not take multiple loans beyond a threshold. Transparency and fair practices to protect borrowers from harassment.

questions.question: Which among the following Mughal Emperors propounded a new way of life/religion called Din-i-Ilahi?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Akbar. The Mughal Emperor Akbar propounded a new way of life called Din-i-Ilahi in 1582. It was a syncretic religion aimed at uniting the best elements of various faiths, including Islam, Hinduism, Christianity, Jainism, and Zoroastrianism. Akbar introduced it to promote religious harmony in his empire. However, Din-i-Ilahi was more of a spiritual and ethical code than an organised religion and had very few followers. It reflected Akbar's policy of tolerance and his interest in interfaith dialogue.

questions.question: Mahatma Gandhi started the Salt March on 12 March 1930 from:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Sabarmati. • Beginning of Salt March: Mahatma Gandhi began the Salt March (also known as the Dandi March) on 12 March 1930 from the Sabarmati Ashram in Ahmedabad, Gujarat. • Purpose: The march was a direct action against the British salt laws, which imposed a tax even on natural salt, making it illegal for Indians to collect or sell salt independently. • Dandi March: Gandhi walked about 240 kilometers from Sabarmati to the coastal village of Dandi in Gujarat. The journey took 24 days, and on 6 April 1930, he broke the salt law by making salt from seawater. • Significance: The Salt March became a pivotal moment in the Indian freedom struggle, drawing massive public support and international attention. • Peaceful Protest: Based on the principles of Satyagraha (truth-force) and non-violence, the march showcased Gandhi’s strategy of civil disobedience and inspired countless Indians to join the movement for independence.

questions.question: Who among the following was/were hanged for killing British officers Walter Charles Rand and Charles Ayerst in the year 1897?
options: Ram Prasad Bismil and Ashfaqullah Khan
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Chapekar brothers. Here are the key points related to their revolutionary action in 1897: • Year of the Incident: The event took place in 1897 in Pune during a plague outbreak. • Assassination of British Officers: The Chapekar brothers assassinated British officers Walter Charles Rand and Charles Ayerst. • Reason: The British officer Rand was accused of treating Indians inhumanely and disrespectfully under the guise of plague control measures. • Key Revolutionaries: Damodar Chapekar (leader), along with his brothers Balakrishna and Vasudev Chapekar. • Details of the Attack: On 23rd June 1897, the brothers ambushed and shot the officers while they were returning in a horse-drawn carriage. • Aftermath: All three brothers were arrested by the British authorities and later executed by hanging. • Significance: This marked one of the earliest revolutionary acts in India’s freedom struggle and inspired many young Indians to join the fight against British colonial rule.

questions.question: Where did the infamous Jallianwala Bagh massacre take place in 1919?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Amritsar. • Jallianwala Bagh Massacre (1919): This incident took place on 13th April 1919 in Amritsar, Punjab. It is one of the most infamous events in Indian history, remembered as a dark chapter in the struggle for independence. • Details of the Incident: • British Army Officer, General Dyer ordered the firing on a peaceful gathering at Jallianwala Bagh. People had assembled there for a peaceful protest against the British colonial rule. • Without any warning, General Dyer opened fire on the crowd, and it continued for about 10 minutes. • It is estimated that around 379 people were killed, and over 1,000 were injured, although the British government underreported these figures. • Significance: • This massacre shocked the Indian population and became a turning point in the Indian freedom struggle. • The massacre spurred widespread anger and rebellion, further fueling the movement for India's independence. • The Jallianwala Bagh massacre became a major catalyst for protests and resistance against British rule in India. • Jallianwala Bagh Memorial: • Today, Jallianwala Bagh has a memorial where people pay homage to those who lost their lives in this horrific event. It serves as a reminder of the sacrifices made by the people during the freedom struggle.

questions.question: Who among the following said that Raziyya was more able and /qualified than all her brothers for the Sultan of Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Minhaj-i Siraj. It was Minhaj-i-Siraj, a prominent Persian historian and chronicler of the Delhi Sultanate, who said that Raziyya (Razia Sultana) was more able and qualified than all her brothers to rule as the Sultan. • Minhaj-i-Siraj was the author of "Tabaqat-i Nasiri", a historical chronicle. • He praised Raziyya for her intelligence, courage, and administrative skills. • Despite his conservative views on gender roles, he acknowledged that she was better suited for the throne than her male siblings.

questions.question: Which of the following censuses recorded the highest gap in male and female literacy rates in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 2011. The 2011 Census recorded the highest gap in male and female literacy rates in India, with Rajasthan showing the largest disparity at 27.85% (male literacy: 80.51%, female literacy: 52.66%). Key Findings from the 2011 Census: 1. National Average Gap: 16.68% (Male: 82.14%, Female: 65.46%). 2. State-wise Extremes: • Rajasthan (Highest Gap): 27.85% . • Kerala (Lowest Gap): 4.5% (Female literacy surpassed male literacy in Meghalaya). 3. Other States with High Gaps: • Bihar: 20.7% (NFHS-5 data, post-2011). • Telangana: 18.2%. Contextual Trends: • The gap has narrowed since 1981 (23% gap) due to initiatives like the Right to Education Act (2009). • Persistent Challenges: Rural areas (e.g., Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh) face deeper disparities due to child marriage, lack of menstrual hygiene infrastructure, and gendered labor roles.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a lagoon lake in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Dal lake. Dal Lake is located in Jammu and Kashmir and is not a lagoon lake. It is a freshwater lake, known for its beauty and houseboats. It is situated in the Kashmir Valley and is an important part of the region’s ecosystem and tourism. Other options: • Chilika Lake: Chilika is a lagoon lake, situated along the coast of Odisha, and is the largest coastal lagoon in India. It is a significant wetland and an important habitat for migratory birds. • Pulicat Lake: Pulicat is another lagoon lake located on the Andhra Pradesh-Tamil Nadu border, near the Bay of Bengal. It is the second-largest brackish water lake in India. • Vembanad Lake: Vembanad is also a lagoon lake, situated in Kerala. It is the longest lake in India and is known for its scenic beauty and as the site of the famous annual Nehru Trophy Boat Race.

questions.question: Van Vihar National Park is located in which of the following cities of India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Bhopal • Van Vihar National Park is located in Bhopal, the capital of Madhya Pradesh in central India. • This National Park was declared a national park in 1979, it is spread over an area of ​​about 4.45 sq km. • There are the following 11 National Parks in Madhya Pradesh which are mentioned below: • Kanha kisli National Park 1955 • Madhav National Park 1958 • Bandhavqarh National Park 1968 • Pench National Park 1975 • Van National Park 1979 • Saniay National Park 1981 • Panna National Park 1981 • Fossil National Park 1983 • Satpura National Park 1981 • Bagh fossil National Park 2010 • Kuno-Palpur Park 2018

questions.question: There are four seasons experienced in India and each season has its significance. Which of the following is known as the rainy season?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Advancing monsoon. The Advancing Monsoon (also called the Southwest Monsoon) is the rainy season in India, occurring from June to September. It plays a vital role in India's climate and economy, especially in agriculture, as most of the country's rainfall is received during this period. The monsoon onset occurs in Kerala around June 1. The monsoon is driven by southwest winds that originate over the Indian Ocean and bring moisture-laden air to the subcontinent.

questions.question: Which of the following regions of India does NOT have a clearly-defined winter season?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Peninsular India. Peninsular India does NOT have a clearly-defined winter season because of its tropical climate and proximity to the equator. The region experiences moderate temperatures throughout the year. Winters are mild, with only a slight drop in temperature. There is no distinct cold season as seen in northern India, where temperatures can drop significantly during winter.

questions.question: What is the kinetic energy of an object of mass 10kg moving with velocity 2 metre/second?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 20 J • Kinetic energy is a form of energy that an object or a particle has by reason of its motion. • KE = 1/2 mv2 • M = Mass of the body • V = Velocity of body • KE = Kinetic energy concept Given that Mass of the body(m) = 10 kg speed of the object (v) = 2 m/s KE = 1/2(10)(2)2 KE= 20 joules

questions.question: In second law of motion F = ma, F stands for
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Net external force on body. Newton's Second Law of Motion, F = ma, forms the foundation for understanding any motion. Here, F refers to the net external force acting on an object — that is, the total force coming from external sources that has the potential to change the object's motion. This is the very force that produces acceleration in the object. Internal forces (like forces between atoms) or forces that cancel each other out do not change motion and are therefore not included in this equation. This law is fundamental in fields like science, engineering, and robotics, as it teaches that only unbalanced external forces truly matter.

questions.question: Which part of the digestive system is responsible for the absorption of vitamin B12?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Ileum. • The ileum, the final part of the small intestine, is specifically responsible for the absorption of vitamin B12. • Vitamin B12 binds with intrinsic factor (a glycoprotein produced in the stomach) and is absorbed in the ileum through receptor-mediated endocytosis. • A deficiency in vitamin B12 can lead to conditions such as pernicious anemia and neurological issues. The ileum's ability to absorb vitamin B12 is critical for maintaining adequate levels of this vitamin in the body.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true regarding the phylum Arthropoda? • Arthropods have an exoskeleton made of chitin. • They undergo a process of metamorphosis in their life cycle. • Arthropods are exclusively aquatic organisms. • They possess jointed appendages and segmented bodies.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 1, 2, and 4 are correct. • Arthropods have an exoskeleton made of chitin – This is correct. Arthropods possess an external skeleton (exoskeleton) made of chitin, which provides protection, and structural support, and prevents desiccation. • They undergo a process of metamorphosis in their life cycle – This is correct. Many arthropods, particularly insects, undergo metamorphosis, which can be complete (egg → larva → pupa → adult) or incomplete (egg → nymph → adult), depending on the species. • Arthropods are exclusively aquatic organisms – This is incorrect. While many arthropods, such as crustaceans (e.g., crabs, lobsters), are aquatic, a large number of arthropods, especially insects (e.g., butterflies, ants), are terrestrial. Therefore, arthropods are not exclusively aquatic. • They possess jointed appendages and segmented bodies – This is correct. Arthropods have jointed limbs, which are a key feature that distinguishes them from other invertebrates. Their bodies are also segmented, often into distinct regions like the head, thorax, and abdomen (in insects), or cephalothorax and abdomen (in arachnids).

questions.question: Which of the following is also called sperm duct ?
questions.explanation: • Correct answer is option 3 • Vas deferens is about 30 cm long tube which begins from the tail of epididymis . It is joined with the duct of seminal vesicle . • after maturity sperms are carried by the vas deferens or sperm duct .

questions.question: Match the following animals with their unique characteristics: Column A Column B 1. Frog A) External fertilization 2. Eagle B) Oviparous with a hard shell 3. Seahorse C) Aerial adaptation with sharp talons 4. Cockroach D) The male carries eggs in a brood pouch
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 1-A, 2-C, 3-D, 4-B. • Frog – Frogs are amphibians that exhibit external fertilization, where the female lays eggs in water, and the male fertilizes them externally. • Eagle – Eagles are birds of prey, and their primary aerial adaptation includes sharp talons for catching and gripping prey, and strong wings for soaring through the sky. • Seahorse – Seahorses are unique among fish because males carry eggs in a brood pouch. The female seahorse deposits eggs into the male’s pouch, where fertilization occurs, and the male carries the developing young. • Cockroach – Cockroaches are oviparous, meaning they lay eggs. Their eggs are enclosed in a hard, protective structure called an ootheca, which shields the eggs until they hatch.

questions.question: What is the number of electrons present in magnesium (atomic mass = 24)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 12. The number of electrons in a neutral atom is equal to its atomic number. The atomic mass (24 in this case) tells us the approximate total number of protons and neutrons, but it's the atomic number that determines the number of protons and, consequently, the number of electrons in a neutral atom. For Magnesium (Mg), its atomic number is 12. Therefore, a neutral Magnesium atom has 12 protons and 12 electrons. Other options: • 24: This is the approximate atomic mass, not the number of electrons. • 10: This would be the number of electrons if it were a Magnesium ion with a +2 charge (Mg²⁺), but the question asks for a neutral atom. • 16: This number does not correspond to the atomic number of Magnesium or any common charge state.

questions.question: What is the taste of acid?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3, i.e., sour. Acids in general taste kind of sour (think about citric acid in citrus fruits), and bases, in general, taste bitter or even soapy.

questions.question: Which of the following is not correct?
options: Telnet lets user to access an application on a remote computer
Telnet is a general purpose client-server program
Telnet can be used for remote login
Telnet can also be used for file tranfer
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Telnet can also be used for file transfer File Transfer Protocol is used to transfer files. Telnet provides access to the command-line interface on a remote host. Telnet is an application protocol used on the Internet or local area network to provide a bidirectional interactive text-oriented communication facility using a virtual terminal connection.

questions.question: Which among the following is the term used when a search engine returns to a Web page that matches the search criteria?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Hit. • Hit is the term used when a search engine returns a Web page that matches the search criteria. • A search engine is a tool which matches keywords we enter with titles and description on the Internet. • It then displays the matches allowing to easily locate a subject. Similar to a card catalog, but not as efficient. • Common search engines are Web crawler, Yahoo, Alta Vista, Infoseek, Google, and Lycos.

questions.question: Which of the following is the technology that is used for collecting data from “fill-in-the-bubble” forms?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. OMR. • OMR is a popular recognition technology is used for collecting data from “fill-in-the-bubble” forms such as educational tests, surveys, assessments, evaluations, and many other multiple-choice forms. • OMR stands for Optical Mark Recognition. • It is considered to be the fastest, efficient, accurate means of conducting any examination. • OCR stands for Optical Character Recognition.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of Algorithmic language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. All of the above. • Algorithmic languages are designed to express mathematical or symbolic computations. • They can express algebraic operations in a notation similar to mathematics and allow the use of subprograms that package commonly used operations for reuse. • Algorithmic languages were the first high-level languages. • Some of the examples of Algorithmic languages are: • FORTRAN • ALGOL • LISP • C

questions.question: In which state of India is the Khajuraho dance festival celebrated?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Madhya Pradesh. • The 49th Khajuraho Dance Festival began with Bharatnatyam and Kathak in Madhya Pradesh at the temple and was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site. • The Khajuraho Dance Festival (Khajuraho Nritya Samaroh), organized by the Madhya Pradesh Culture Department through the Ustad Alauddin Khan Music and Arts Academy in Bhopal, The festival was hosted each year at the magnificent city of Khajuraho, located in the district of Chhatarpur. • The Khajuraho Group of Monuments is a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its Nagara-style architecture. • It first began in 1975. • Celebrated every year in February.

questions.question: In which state of India is the Nuakhai festival celebrated?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Odisha. Nuakhai is a significant agricultural festival celebrated with immense joy, primarily in the Indian state of Odisha, especially in its western regions. The name "Nuakhai" literally translates to "new food" (Nua = new, Khai = food/eat), signifying the harvesting of the fresh rice crop. Explanation of Other Options: • Bihar: Prominently celebrates Chhath Puja. • Assam: Famous for its Bihu harvest festivals. • Tamil Nadu: Celebrates Pongal as its main harvest festival.

questions.question: Guru Gopinath is related to which folk dance form?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Katahakali. Guru Gopinath is associated with Kathakali, a classical dance-drama from Kerala known for its elaborate costumes, facial makeup, and storytelling through expressive gestures. He played a key role in popularizing Kathakali beyond temple stages and modernizing its presentation for broader audiences. Other Options: • Ponung is a traditional folk dance of Arunachal Pradesh, performed by the Adi tribe women before the harvest season to seek blessings. • Kathak is a classical dance form from North India, marked by intricate footwork and spins. Birju Maharaj is a legendary maestro of the Kathak dance form. • Manipuri originates from Manipur and is devotional, graceful, and centered around themes from Vaishnavism, especially Radha-Krishna stories. Guru Bipin Singh was a prominent exponent of Manipuri classical dance.

questions.question: Mahasu is the folk dance of which state?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. Himachal Pradesh. • Mahasu, Jhora, Jnali are the folk dances of Himachal Pradesh. • Kuchipudi is the folk dance of Andhra Pradesh. • Kumi & Kavadi are the folk dances of Tamil Nadu. • Bihu, Bichchua, Natpuja, Maharas are the famous folk dances of Assam.

questions.question: Which Indian musician won a Grammy twice for Danny Boyle’s film Slumdog Millionaire in 2010?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. AR Rahman. • Rahman had won two Academy Awards in 2009 for Danny Boyle’s Slumdog Millionaire. • He won the award for both Best Original Score and Best Original Song for the single Jai Ho. • In 2008, Rahman's work gained global prominence with the extraordinary success of his score for 'Slumdog Millionaire' that won 8 Academy Awards including two for Rahman, for Best Score and Best Song. • Rahman won over 15 awards for this score including two Grammys, the Golden Globe and the BAFTA.

questions.question: "Dreams From My Father" is an autobiography of ______________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Barack Obama. "Dreams From My Father: A Story of Race and Inheritance" is an autobiography by Barack Obama, who later became the 44th President of the United States of America. • Content: The book covers his early life, including his childhood in Hawaii, his relationship with his absent Kenyan father (Barack Obama Sr.), his mother's family, and his experiences as a community organizer in Chicago. It explores themes of race, identity, and family. • Publication: The book was first published in 1995, long before his presidency, and gained significant attention during his political rise. Additional Information about other options • Benito Mussolini: An Italian politician and journalist who founded and led the National Fascist Party. His autobiography is titled "My Autobiography." • Anne Frank: A Jewish girl who hid with her family during the Holocaust and kept a diary. Her famous work is "The Diary of a Young Girl" (also known as "The Diary of Anne Frank"). • Benjamin Franklin: One of the Founding Fathers of the United States. His autobiography, often simply called "The Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin," is a classic of the genre and details his life from 1706 to 1757.

questions.question: The term Stumping is related to which of the following sports?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e.Cricket • Stumping is a mode of dismissal in cricket performed by the wicketkeeper. • It occurs when the wicketkeeper puts down the wicket while the batsman is out of his crease and not attempting a run. • Stumping requires quick reflexes and is often seen when the batsman steps forward to play a delivery but misses the ball. • It is different from a run-out, as stumping happens only off a delivery and with the batsman not attempting a run. Additional info about Other Sports • Basketball: A team sport where two teams of five players try to score by shooting a ball through the opponent’s hoop. • Tennis: A racket sport played individually or in pairs, where players hit a ball over a net to score points. • Baseball: A bat-and-ball game played between two teams of nine players, aiming to score runs by hitting a pitched ball and running bases.

questions.question: The term "LBW" Is related to which sport?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Cricket • The term "LBW" is related to Cricket. • "LBW" stands for "Leg Before Wicket". • LBW is one of the 10 ways a batter can be dismissed in cricket. • It first appeared in cricket rules in 1774.
